Colombo, Sri Lanka
Colombo, Sri Lanka
Day 01Arrival · Colombo

Arrival on the Island's Western Shore

Bandaranaike International Airport → Colombo

Your Sri Lankan journey begins at Bandaranaike International Airport, where our representative will meet you before your transfer into Colombo. The drive into the city gives you a first look at the coast, with the Indian Ocean alongside you for much of the way.

Colombo is Sri Lanka's commercial capital and its most cosmopolitan city, colonial buildings sit beside busy markets, and a modern skyline along the seafront. Portuguese, Dutch and British influence over the centuries has given the city a character shaped by trade and the mixing of cultures.

The afternoon is set aside for the Rotary Club project and institution visits, a chance to meet local community initiatives and see the social work being done on the ground. It's a good way to begin and sets the tone for the days that follow.

The rest of the evening is free to settle in and recover from your flight.

For dinner, we'd suggest Ministry of Crab, set in Colombo's old Dutch Hospital building and a regular on Asia's 50 Best Restaurants list. Sri Lankan lagoon crab is among the finest anywhere, and this is the place to eat it — sorted by size and served with pepper, chilli or the well-known garlic butter. Worth booking well ahead.

🚗1 hour

Where you'll stay · First night

1/6

Shangri-La, Colombo

Day 1

Rising above the green sweep of Galle Face Green, Shangri-La, Colombo commands the city's seafront with quiet confidence. Interiors marry contemporary elegance with Sri Lankan craft — latticed screens, hand-woven textures and warm island light — across a landmark tower that gazes out over the Indian Ocean. A rooftop pool, an acclaimed collection of restaurants and bars, and generous ocean-view rooms make it a polished place to begin the journey, with the gracious, unhurried service for which Colombo is known.

City Hotel
Tea Country, Ella
Day 02Colombo · Ella

Into the Emerald Hill Country

Colombo → Ella

This morning you board a scenic flight to the hill country. It's a fine way to take in Sri Lanka's change in landscape, as the coastal plains give way to tea estates, deep valleys and mountain ridges wrapped in mist.

On arrival, check in and spend the rest of the day at leisure. With all the essentials and the finer details already taken care of, you can simply enjoy the property. Gourmet breakfasts, lunches and dinners are prepared with freshly sourced local ingredients, and select wines, beer and spirits, along with fruit juices and soft drinks, are all included.

In the afternoon, you'll head out for an artisanal tea factory experience curated with Uga Ghiri. Set in the heart of tea country, this is a hands-on look at how Ceylon tea makes its way from bush to cup — through withering, rolling, oxidation and firing, with each stage explained as you go.

You'll try your hand at rolling the leaf yourself, learning the technique that shapes the tea and draws out its character. The session finishes with a guided tasting, comparing different grades and picks side by side, and learning how to pick out the differences in colour, aroma and strength. It's an enjoyable couple of hours, and a good way to understand why Ceylon tea has the reputation it does.

✈️Scenic Flight ⏱️approx. 55 min 🍽️All-Inclusive

Where you'll stay · First night

1/7

Uga Ghiri, Ella

Days 2–3 · All-Inclusive

Perched high in the mist near Ella, Uga Ghiri is a contemporary sanctuary folded into the tea country's ridges and valleys. Its villa suites open through full-height glass to sweeping highland views, each with a private plunge pool, sculptural bathroom and a calm, tactile palette of timber, stone and linen. At its heart, a striking wrought-iron atrium wrapped around a living tree gives way to a colonnaded terrace and an intimate billiards bar. All-inclusive and effortlessly serene, it is the hill country distilled into a single address.

All-Inclusive · Hill-Country Retreat
Ella, Hill Country
Day 03Based in Ella

The Bridge in the Sky

A morning on the Ella–Badulla line

This morning, you'll take one of the world's best-known train rides — the run from Ella to Badulla, around 45 minutes to an hour. The line passes through tunnels, tea gardens and forested slopes, with the view from the window changing constantly.

Along the way, the train passes over the Nine Arch Bridge and through the Demodara Loop, a clever piece of colonial-era engineering where the track spirals underneath itself to manage the gradient. Do bear in mind that you won't see a great deal of either from on board, as the train moves through quickly — the appeal is in the journey itself and the scenery either side, rather than the landmarks.

In the afternoon, the team will put together a tasting of tea-infused cocktails and mocktails, made with specialty Ceylon teas blended with spirits and fresh local ingredients. Each one is talked through as it's served, so you get a sense of how the different teas come through in the glass.

Hosting the session is Mr. Salinda Madugalle, a retired tea planter who started out in 1977 and spent more than thirty years in the tea fields, later working as a consultant across a few estates in the hill country. He'll share stories from his years as a planter — what life on the estates was like back then, and how things have changed since. It's a relaxed way to end the day, and a nice way to make dinner.

🚂Train ~1 hr 🍃Teaology 🍽️All-Inclusive

Tonight's Stay

Uga Ghiri, Ella

Day 3 · All-Inclusive

Yala National Park
Day 04Ella · Yala

Down to the Wild South-East

Ella → Yala, via Wellawaya

This morning you head to Yala, dropping down from the hills towards the dry southeastern plains. The route through Wellawaya is a scenic one, with tea slopes giving way to scrubland, paddy fields and rocky outcrops — a good illustration of how quickly the landscape changes here.

In the afternoon, you'll head out on a game drive in Yala National Park. Yala is Sri Lanka's bestknown wildlife reserve, with one of the highest leopard densities anywhere in the world. There are also elephants, sloth bears, spotted deer, wild buffalo, crocodiles and a lot of birdlife, set against thorny scrub, lagoons, rock formations and coastal dunes.

Late afternoon is one of the better times for sightings, as animals come out to feed and gather at waterholes. You'll be with an experienced tracker and naturalist, exploring the park by jeep. Do make sure your camera is fully charged with it. There is plenty worth photographing, and with luck you may spot one of the leopards the park is known for.

The park is also home to a good range of endemic and migratory birds. Keep an eye out for the Sri Lanka grey hornbill, Sri Lanka jungle fowl, Sri Lanka wood pigeon, crimson-fronted barbet, blackcapped bulbul, blue-tailed bee-eater and brown-capped babbler.

The drive lasts around two and a half to three hours, after which you'll return to the camp to freshen up.

  • Start times: 14:30 for Block 1
  • Duration: 2.5 – 3 hours
  • Maximum six guests per drive

There's sundown in the afternoon to enjoy with your colleagues.

Tonight, dinner is al fresco out in the wilderness. Dining by the beach, with the Indian Ocean close by, makes for an atmospheric and intimate end to the day.

🐆Game Drive 🍽️All-Inclusive

Where you'll stay · First night

1/7

Wild Coast Tented Lodge, Yala

Day 4 · All-Inclusive

Where the jungle of Yala meets the surf of the Indian Ocean, Wild Coast Tented Lodge is one of Sri Lanka's most extraordinary places to stay. Cocoon-like tented suites — their bamboo-domed architecture rising among granite boulders and coastal thicket — come with private plunge pools and gleaming copper bathtubs. The soaring bamboo-vaulted clubhouse and boulder-set pool blur the line between design and wilderness, while leopards and elephants roam the reserve at the doorstep. All-inclusive and thrillingly wild, it is a safari lodge quite unlike any other.

All-Inclusive · Tented Safari Lodge
Weligama, South Coast
Day 05Yala · Weligama

Along the Southern Shore

Yala → Weligama

This afternoon, transfer along the south coast to Weligama, around two and a half to three hours. The coastal road is a pleasure, with palm trees, fishing villages, wide bays and stretches of sand, and the ocean in view for much of the way.

Weligama means "sandy village", and it's known for its wide crescent bay, gentle surf and the stilt fishermen along the coast nearby.

You'll arrive and check in at Cape Weligama, set on a headland high above the Indian Ocean and one of the island's best-known resorts. It's a Relais & Châteaux property, with villas that come with their own pools, wide ocean views from almost everywhere, and a quiet, easy feel to it.

As the sun goes down, there's the Moon Pool, an infinity pool on the clifftop or you can simply wander the gardens for a while before dinner.

Dinner is at the Ocean Terrace Restaurant, where the menu leans on local seafood alongside international dishes, with the ocean stretching out in front of you.

🌊South-Coast Drive 🚗2.5–3 hrs 🍽️Half Board

Where you'll stay · First night

1/9

Cape Weligama

Days 5–6 · Half Board

Set on a dramatic headland high above the south-coast surf, Cape Weligama is a Resplendent Ceylon resort of rare romance. Its signature crescent infinity pool traces the cliff edge above the ocean, while individually styled villas and suites — many with private pools — look out to an endless blue horizon. Days drift between the thatched beach bar, ocean-view dining terraces and the sound of waves far below, and the sunsets here are the stuff of memory. Gracious, intimate and quietly glamorous, it is a fitting finale to any Sri Lankan journey.

Half Board · Cliff-top Resort
Mirissa, Southern Ocean
Day 06Weligama · Mirissa · Weligama

Giants of the Deep, a Farewell by the Waves

Weligama → Mirissa → Weligama

This morning, you'll head out whale watching by private yacht from Mirissa. The waters off the south coast are among the best in the world for seeing blue whales, as the continental shelf drops away steeply close to shore and brings them within easy reach. Sperm whales, spinner dolphins and flying fish are common too, and having the yacht to yourselves means you can take your time out on the water.

The afternoon is given over to a conference session, or alternatively a guest lecture on the Ayurvedic approach to immunity. Ayurveda has been practised in Sri Lanka for more than two thousand years, and its focus on balance, prevention and local herbs is still very much part of everyday life here.

This evening, there's a farewell beach dinner — a good way to finish the trip, with the sound of the waves nearby.

🐋Whale Watching ⛵Private Yacht 🍽️Half Board

Tonight's Stay

Cape Weligama

Day 6 · Half Board

South Coast → Colombo
Day 07Departure

A Last Look at the Shore

Weligama → Bandaranaike International Airport

After breakfast and a last free morning on the south coast, you'll be transferred to Bandaranaike International Airport in good time for your flight.

You head home with memories of a varied trip and, we hope, with plans to return.

🚗2 hr 30 min 🍽️Breakfast

Your journey concludes here — we wish you safe onward travels.

Journey Complete

Until We Meet Again in Sri Lanka

From the cosmopolitan energy of Colombo to the misty tea estates and celebrated railway of the hill country, from the leopards of Yala to the great blue whales off Mirissa and the cliff-top sunsets of Weligama, this has been a journey through the very best of the island. It has been our privilege to craft these seven days for you, and our hope that they leave you with memories to carry home — and a reason to return.

We look forward to welcoming you on this unforgettable journey.
